title: Implement kubernetes secret handling for podman play kube
body:
add support for both creating a secret using yaml and mounting a secret as a volume given a yaml file.
Kubernetes secrets have a different structure than podman and therefore have to be handeled differently.
In this PR, I have introduced the basic usecases of kube secrets with more implementations like env secrets to come!

resolves #12396

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cdoern@redhat.com>

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

```release-note
podman now supports kubernetes secrets in podman play kube
```

comments.body: @vrothberg @ashley-cui @rhatdan @umohnani8 PTAL
@ashley-cui @vrothberg what do you think about the name issue?
I think the best approach here is leaving the secrets with unique names. Either way, this change will need to be made in c/common not podman
@vrothberg @ashley-cui ping

I'd tend to agree, as Kubernetnes allows separate files for secrets and the actual kube yaml, as well as a combined file. If we were to name the secret with a hash of the combined file, we would not be able to support separate files.
@flouthoc thanks for the review! @ashley-cui @vrothberg WDYT about replacing a secret when using the same name? I could wire that up in c/common.

I think this would have to be kube specfic logic, as we still don't want podman secrets to be mutable. I'm wondering if its a better idea to not wire this in common, but rather only on the kube side, where we would check if a kube secret exists and delete it and re-create it if the name collides.
I concur with Ashley.  Nothing IMHO should be changed in c/common.
@ashley-cui @vrothberg as far as the immutable option that was brought up, that would need to be wired into c/common somehow right? or maybe we could write the metadata?
@cdoern @ashley-cui @vrothberg is this ready for podman 4.2?
@rhatdan, @vrothberg thinks it is best if we include this in 4.3
Ok we can wait til then
Since we branched for 4.2 already, we are not blocked.

/approved
@vrothberg Does this LGTY? @ashley-cui ?
@ashley-cui @vrothberg should I get metadata handling into common before merging this? probably right?
@cdoern, yes, should a a quick PR :)

/hold 
until I get a common Pr and rebase this, it has been sitting about a week

/hold cancel
@vrothberg @ashley-cui PTAL, vendored in c/common with my new changes.
Which PR are we holding up on containers/common?
@rhatdan, it already merged. it was the metadata PR containers/common#1096
@vrothberg @ashley-cui good to go here?
LGTM
I will do a last review tomorrow morning. My brain is already fried for today :)
@flouthoc this is waiting for your approval I believe since you requested changes.
